tweets: The standard listing appointment structure works against you.
Most operators go in with a prepared presentation and a plan to handle objections. That structure assumes the owner needs to be convinced.
The owners who are self-managing or leaving a prior manager are usually not unconvinced. They are specifically unconvinced that you understand their situation.
The attunement approach: reduce your power posture, ask more than you tell, and reflect the owner's situation back before you pitch anything.
Let the owner describe the problem your proposal will solve. When they do, you're not convincing them. You're confirming something they already feel.
The operator who helps an owner see their situation more clearly than they did walking in wins on authority. Not on features and not on price.
